walkthrough.title: Choose Your Chapter
walkthrough.text: Let's get Mice and Mystics set up. First, we need to decide which chapter we are playing. If this is our first time, we will start with Chapter One. Open the storybook to the chapter we have chosen and read the introduction aloud. This sets the scene and tells us what our heroes are up to.

walkthrough.title: Select the Room Tiles
walkthrough.text: Now look at the chapter setup section in the storybook. It will list which room tiles we need for this adventure. Find those double-sided tiles and set them nearby, but do not lay them all out yet. We only place the starting tile for now.

walkthrough.title: Place the Starting Tile
walkthrough.text: Take the starting room tile indicated by the chapter and place it in the center of the table. This is where our mice begin their adventure. Make sure the correct side is face up as specified in the storybook.

walkthrough.title: Place Tokens on the Starting Tile
walkthrough.text: The chapter setup tells us exactly which tokens to place on the starting tile. Add search tokens to the spaces indicated. These represent items and secrets our mice can discover during the adventure. Also place any mouse holes or special markers as described.

walkthrough.title: Set Up Starting Minions
walkthrough.text: The chapter setup also tells us which enemy minions start on the board. Place the indicated miniatures on the spaces described. Common starting enemies include rats, roaches, and centipedes. Make sure we have the right number for our player count, since minion counts often scale.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Page Track
walkthrough.text: Find the page track board and place the page marker on the starting position indicated by the chapter. This track is our timer. Every time the minion cheese wheel fills up, the page marker advances. If it reaches the end before we complete the objective, we lose the chapter.

walkthrough.title: Set the Cheese Wheel to Zero
walkthrough.text: Place the cheese wheel marker at zero. Throughout the game, whenever minions roll cheese symbols on their dice, cheese gets added to this shared wheel. When it fills up completely, bad things happen: new minions appear and the page advances. Think of it as the villain's momentum tracker.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Card Decks
walkthrough.text: Shuffle the search card deck and place it face down near the board. These are the items and treasures our mice can discover. Also gather the encounter cards for this chapter and the initiative cards. Set aside any story cards that the chapter references.

walkthrough.title: Choose Your Mice
walkthrough.text: Now each player picks a mouse hero. Some chapters require specific mice, so check the storybook first. The six heroes are Collin the warrior, Maginos the wizard, Tilda the healer, Nez the tinker, Filch the scamp, and Lily the archer. Each mouse has different stats and unique abilities, so pick someone who sounds fun.

walkthrough.title: Take Your Hero Card and Miniature
walkthrough.text: Grab your chosen mouse's hero card and matching miniature. The hero card shows your mouse's stats: battle value for melee attacks, defense value, movement speed, and maximum health. Place the miniature on the starting tile in the space indicated by the chapter.

walkthrough.title: Collect Ability Cards and Equipment
walkthrough.text: Take your mouse's starting ability cards as listed on the hero card. If we are continuing a campaign from a previous chapter, also grab any items that carried over. Each mouse starts with specific abilities that define their role on the team. Set these cards face up in front of you.

walkthrough.title: Set Health and Cheese
walkthrough.text: Set your mouse's health to its maximum value using the health tracker. Each mouse also starts with zero personal cheese tokens. We will earn cheese during the game by rolling cheese symbols on our dice. Cheese fuels our special abilities, so we want to collect it.

walkthrough.title: Determine Initiative Order
walkthrough.text: Gather the initiative cards for all the mice heroes in play and for each minion type that starts on the board. Arrange these cards to determine turn order. Heroes and minion groups will alternate turns based on this initiative order throughout the chapter.

walkthrough.title: Read the Chapter Objective
walkthrough.text: Before we begin, let's make sure everyone knows what we are trying to accomplish. Read the chapter objective aloud one more time. It might be rescuing an ally, reaching a specific location, or defeating a boss. Keep this goal in mind with every decision we make. Ready? Let's play!

summary.title: Prepare the Card Decks

Gather encounter cards, initiative cards, and story cards for the chapter

summary.title: Choose Your Mice
bullets: Each player picks a mouse hero (some chapters require specific mice)
6 heroes: Collin (warrior), Maginos (wizard), Tilda (healer), Nez (tinker), Filch (scamp), Lily (archer)

summary.title: Take Your Hero Card and Miniature
bullets: Grab hero card showing stats: battle, defense, speed, health
Place miniature on starting tile per chapter instructions

summary.title: Collect Ability Cards and Equipment
bullets: Take starting ability cards listed on hero card
If continuing campaign, grab carried-over items

summary.title: Set Health and Cheese
bullets: Set health to maximum on tracker
Start with 0 personal cheese tokens

summary.title: Determine Initiative Order
bullets: Gather initiative cards for all heroes and minion types
Arrange to determine turn order for the chapter

summary.title: Read the Chapter Objective
bullets: Read the chapter objective aloud so all players know the goal
Objectives: rescue allies, reach locations, defeat bosses, etc.

walkthrough.title: Round Structure
walkthrough.text: Mice and Mystics is played in rounds. Each round, every hero and every minion group takes one turn in initiative order. Heroes and minion groups alternate, so the flow might be: mouse hero, rat group, mouse hero, roach group, and so on. When every character has taken a turn, the round ends and we check for any end-of-round effects before starting the next round.

walkthrough.title: Hero Turn Overview
walkthrough.text: On our turn, our mouse hero can do two things: move and take one action. We can do these in any order. We can move then act, act then move, or even split our movement around our action. If we do not want to take an action at all, we can scurry instead, which lets us move a second time equal to our speed.

walkthrough.title: Movement
walkthrough.text: Our mouse can move up to a number of spaces equal to its speed value. Movement is counted space by space on the room tile. We can move through spaces occupied by friendly mice, but we must stop if we enter a space with an enemy minion. Moving through a mouse hole or doorway at the edge of a tile lets us explore a new room.

walkthrough.title: Exploring New Rooms
walkthrough.text: When we move through a mouse hole or doorway, we reveal the next room tile as indicated by the chapter. Place the new tile connected to the current one. The chapter instructions tell us which minions and tokens to place in the new room. Exploring is exciting but also dangerous, because new enemies appear every time we enter a fresh room.

walkthrough.title: Melee Combat
walkthrough.text: To attack an enemy in our space, we roll a number of dice equal to our battle value. Each sword symbol rolled is one hit. The defender then rolls dice equal to its defense value, and each shield symbol blocks one hit. Any unblocked hits deal damage. If an enemy's health reaches zero, it is defeated and removed from the board.

walkthrough.title: Ranged Combat
walkthrough.text: Some mice like Lily and Nez can make ranged attacks. Ranged attacks work similarly to melee, but we count bow symbols instead of sword symbols. We can target enemies in our space or in adjacent spaces, depending on the weapon. Ranged attackers are great for supporting teammates without getting into the thick of combat.

walkthrough.title: Cheese on Dice
walkthrough.text: Here is a crucial detail about dice rolls. Every die has cheese wedge symbols on some faces. Whenever we roll cheese, our hero collects those cheese tokens personally. But when minions roll cheese, it goes onto the shared cheese wheel. Cheese is both our fuel for abilities and the thing that advances the enemy timer, so every roll has stakes on both sides.

walkthrough.title: Using Abilities
walkthrough.text: Each mouse has unique ability cards that cost cheese to activate. Collin might spend two cheese to perform a powerful sword strike. Maginos can spend cheese to cast spells. Tilda uses cheese to heal allies. To use an ability, spend the required cheese tokens from our personal supply and follow the card instructions. Abilities can turn the tide of a tough fight.

walkthrough.title: Searching
walkthrough.text: If our mouse is on a space with a search token, we can use our action to search. Draw a card from the search deck. We might find a weapon, armor, a helpful trinket, or a story event. Found items can be equipped right away or given to another mouse in the same space. Each mouse has a limit on how many items they can carry.

walkthrough.title: Sharing Items and Cheese
walkthrough.text: Teamwork is everything in this game. Mice on the same space can freely share cheese tokens and trade items during either mouse's turn. This means Tilda can hand her cheese to Maginos for a big spell, or Collin can pass a found shield to Nez. Staying close together lets us support each other with resources.

walkthrough.title: The Cheese Wheel and Minion Surges
walkthrough.text: The cheese wheel is the heart of the game's tension. Every time minions roll cheese on their dice, it fills up the cheese wheel. When the wheel reaches its limit, three things happen in order. First, the wheel resets to zero. Second, new minions surge onto the board as described in the chapter. Third, the page marker advances one space on the page track. This is how the clock ticks against us.

walkthrough.title: Minion Turns
walkthrough.text: When it is a minion group's turn, each minion in that group activates. Minions follow simple rules: they move toward the nearest mouse hero and attack if they can reach one. Minions roll dice just like we do, dealing hits with sword symbols and generating cheese for the wheel with cheese symbols. We always roll defense dice when attacked.

walkthrough.title: Getting Captured
walkthrough.text: If a mouse hero's health drops to zero, that mouse is captured. The captured mouse is placed on the chapter page track and misses their turns. Our teammates can rescue us by reaching a specific location or completing a task described in the chapter. But be careful: if all mice are captured at the same time, we lose the chapter immediately.

walkthrough.title: The Page Track and Losing
walkthrough.text: The page track is our ultimate deadline. Every time the cheese wheel fills and a surge happens, the page marker moves forward. If the page marker reaches the final space before we complete the chapter objective, the chapter is lost. We can retry the chapter as many times as we want, so a loss is just a chance to try a new approach.

walkthrough.title: Encounter Cards
walkthrough.text: Some page track positions trigger encounter cards. When the page marker lands on a marked space, we draw an encounter card and resolve it. Encounters can bring surprise enemies, story twists, or special events. These add narrative drama and unpredictability to each chapter.

walkthrough.title: Winning a Chapter
walkthrough.text: We win by completing the chapter's story objective before the page track runs out. Objectives vary by chapter: we might need to reach a specific room, defeat a boss enemy, rescue a captured ally, or find a key item. When we complete the objective, we read the victory narrative from the storybook and celebrate together.

walkthrough.title: Campaign Progression
walkthrough.text: After completing a chapter, our mice keep any items they found. Some chapters also grant new ability cards as a reward. The campaign spans eleven chapters telling a continuous story of the mice trying to save their kingdom. Each chapter builds on the last, and our heroes grow stronger over time.

walkthrough.title: Special Die Faces Summary
walkthrough.text: Let's quickly review what each die face means. Swords deal melee damage. Shields block incoming damage. Bows deal ranged damage. And cheese goes to whichever side rolled it: heroes keep their cheese personally, while minion cheese fills the cheese wheel. Every roll in this game matters because both hits and cheese are always possible outcomes.

Check end-of-round effects before starting next round

walkthrough.title: Welcome to Your First Adventure
walkthrough.text: In this practice tutorial, we will walk through the opening turns of Chapter One with a party of four mice: Collin the warrior, Tilda the healer, Nez the tinker, and Filch the scamp. Even if you have fewer players, follow along to see how the game flows. Let's save the kingdom together!

walkthrough.title: The Scene is Set
walkthrough.text: We have set up the starting room tile with our four mice miniatures and a few rat minions as indicated by the chapter. The page track is at its starting position, the cheese wheel is at zero, and each mouse has full health. Our objective for this chapter is to escape the castle dungeon by reaching the final room tile.

walkthrough.title: Collin's Turn: Charge the Rats
walkthrough.text: Collin goes first in initiative order. He moves three spaces toward the nearest rat and attacks. Collin has a battle value of three, so we roll three dice. We get two swords and one cheese. That is two hits! The rat rolls one defense die and gets one shield, blocking one hit. One damage gets through. Collin also collects one cheese token for the cheese he rolled.

walkthrough.title: Why Collin Went First
walkthrough.text: Collin is our warrior and has the highest battle value. By sending him in first, we soften up the enemy before our squishier mice need to act. In a cooperative game, turn order matters a lot. We want our toughest mouse absorbing hits and our ranged attackers staying safely behind.

walkthrough.title: The Mistake: Splitting the Party
walkthrough.text: Filch decides to run ahead toward the mouse hole on the far side of the room, leaving the group behind. He wants to explore the next room and find treasure. But now Filch is alone on the other side of the board, and when the rats activate, they target the nearest mouse. Filch is closest, and he does not have Collin's defense. The rat attacks Filch and deals two damage.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Stay Together
walkthrough.text: Splitting up is the most common mistake new players make in Mice and Mystics. When a mouse is isolated, they cannot receive heals from Tilda, cannot share cheese with allies, and become the closest target for every minion. Always move as a group unless there is a very good reason to send someone ahead.

walkthrough.title: Tilda's Turn: Heal Filch
walkthrough.text: Tilda moves toward Filch and uses her healing ability. She spends one cheese token to restore two health to Filch. This is why Tilda is so important: she keeps the team alive. But notice that Tilda had to spend her entire turn getting to Filch instead of doing something proactive. Filch's reckless move cost the team a turn.

walkthrough.title: Nez's Turn: Ranged Support
walkthrough.text: Nez stays near Collin and uses his ranged attack against the wounded rat. He rolls two dice and gets one bow symbol, dealing one hit. The rat has one health left, so that finishes it off. Nez did not need to move into melee range to help. That is the power of ranged attackers: they contribute to combat while staying safe.

walkthrough.title: Minion Turn: The Rats Strike Back
walkthrough.text: Now it is the rats' turn. Each remaining rat moves toward the nearest mouse and attacks. They roll dice and get one sword and two cheese. The sword deals a hit to Collin, but the two cheese are worse news: they go onto the cheese wheel. The wheel is starting to fill up, and every wedge brings us closer to a surge.

walkthrough.title: The Mistake: Ignoring the Cheese Wheel
walkthrough.text: After a few more rounds, the cheese wheel is nearly full but the party ignores it. They keep fighting every rat they see instead of pushing toward the objective. When the wheel fills, a surge happens: two more rats appear and the page marker advances. The team just lost precious time on the page track because they fought unnecessarily instead of moving toward the exit.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Watch the Clock
walkthrough.text: The page track is the real enemy in Mice and Mystics, not the minions. Every unnecessary fight adds cheese to the wheel and brings us closer to a surge. Sometimes the right move is to run past enemies and head for the mouse hole instead of standing and fighting. We need to balance combat with progress toward our objective.

walkthrough.title: The Mistake: Wasting Cheese on Minor Abilities
walkthrough.text: Collin has three cheese tokens saved up. He decides to use his special ability to deal extra damage to a regular rat that only has one health left. A normal attack would have probably killed it anyway. Now Collin has no cheese left for the boss encounter coming in two rooms. Abilities are powerful but cheese is limited.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Save Cheese for Critical Moments
walkthrough.text: Cheese is our most precious resource. Do not spend it on abilities when a regular attack would do the job. Save cheese for moments that really matter: boss fights, emergency heals when a mouse is about to be captured, or special abilities that help the entire party. Think of cheese as an emergency fund, not pocket change.

walkthrough.title: Searching a Room
walkthrough.text: The party regroups and Filch moves to a space with a search token. He uses his action to search and draws a search card. He finds a piece of equipment that boosts his defense. Filch equips it immediately. Searching is important because good equipment makes future fights much easier. Try to grab search tokens when they are along our path, but do not waste turns backtracking for them.

walkthrough.title: Exploring the Next Room
walkthrough.text: Collin leads the way through the mouse hole into the next room. We place the new room tile, add the indicated search tokens and minions, and read any story text. Two roaches appear in this room. Exploration always brings new enemies, so we want to be prepared before pushing through a doorway.

walkthrough.title: The Mistake: Exploring Before Regrouping
walkthrough.text: Collin rushed through the mouse hole while the rest of the party was still two spaces behind. Now he is alone in a room with two fresh roaches and the party cannot reach him this turn. The roaches will get a free attack on Collin before his allies arrive. Always make sure the whole party is near the exit before the lead mouse explores.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Regroup Before Exploring
walkthrough.text: Before moving through a mouse hole, gather the party together. The ideal pattern is to clear the current room of threats, regroup near the exit, and then explore as a group. The lead mouse goes through first, and the rest follow on their turns. This way we face new enemies as a full team, not one mouse alone.

walkthrough.title: Teamwork in Action
walkthrough.text: Now let's see what good teamwork looks like. The party is grouped together facing the roaches. Collin attacks and wounds one. Nez finishes it with a ranged shot. Tilda shares her cheese with Maginos. Filch searches and finds a useful item. Everyone contributed something different, and the team is stronger for it. That is how we play Mice and Mystics.

walkthrough.title: Continue the Adventure
walkthrough.text: Keep moving through the rooms, following the chapter's story. Remember our key lessons: stay together as a group, watch the cheese wheel and page track, save cheese for big moments, and regroup before exploring new rooms. The objective is always our north star. Every decision should move us closer to completing it.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations
walkthrough.text: You have practiced the core mechanics of Mice and Mystics and seen four common mistakes that new players make. You know how combat works, how to search for items, how the cheese wheel and page track create urgency, and most importantly, how to work as a team. You are ready to take on Chapter One for real.

walkthrough.title: Before You Venture Forth
walkthrough.text:
Before you start your adventure, here are three things that can help.

First, check out General Tips for strategy basics on party composition and cheese management.

Second, reference the Appendix for a quick guide to all the key terms and mechanics.

And most importantly, ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Good luck, brave mice!

walkthrough.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
walkthrough.text: Before we talk strategy, here are the biggest mistakes new players make. First, splitting the party because one player wants to rush ahead or explore solo. Second, fighting every single enemy instead of running past when the page track is getting low. Third, blowing all your cheese on minor abilities early in the chapter instead of saving them for the boss. Fourth, forgetting to search rooms along the way, missing out on equipment that would have helped in later fights. Keep these in mind and you will survive a lot longer.

walkthrough.title: Party Composition
walkthrough.text: Every party needs a balance of roles to succeed. Collin or another melee fighter should be your frontline, taking hits and dealing damage up close. Tilda the healer is nearly essential because she keeps the party alive and reduces the need for risky retreats. Nez or Lily provides ranged damage from safety, and Filch adds utility with his stealth and thievery skills. When choosing your party, make sure you have at least one strong melee fighter and one healer. The third and fourth slots can be flexible based on your playstyle.

walkthrough.title: Cheese Management
walkthrough.text: Cheese is the most strategic resource in the game. Every cheese we collect on our dice is fuel for our abilities, but we only have so many opportunities to earn it. The golden rule is to save cheese for critical moments. A good benchmark is to always keep at least two cheese in reserve on your strongest ability user. Tilda should always have enough cheese to heal in an emergency, and Maginos should always have enough for one big spell. Spend cheese freely only when victory is within reach and the page track has plenty of room.

walkthrough.title: When to Fight and When to Run
walkthrough.text: Not every fight is worth having. Each combat round risks adding cheese to the enemy wheel, which brings surges and advances the page track. Fight when enemies block our path to the objective, when we need to clear a room to search safely, or when we are strong and can win quickly. Run when the page track is getting dangerously low, when enemies are in a room we do not need to stay in, or when we can simply scurry past them to a mouse hole. Learning when to disengage is one of the most important skills in this game.

walkthrough.title: Movement and Positioning
walkthrough.text: Good positioning saves lives. Keep Collin or your melee fighter between enemies and the rest of the party. Position ranged attackers one or two spaces behind the frontline where they can shoot without being targeted. Keep Tilda close enough to heal anyone who needs it but not so close that enemies can reach her. When moving through rooms, move as a group in formation rather than sending mice off in different directions. Think of it like a military escort: the tank leads, the support follows, and nobody wanders off alone.

walkthrough.title: Search Strategy
walkthrough.text: Searching is how we find the weapons, armor, and trinkets that make our mice stronger. Prioritize searching when the token is along our path to the objective so we do not waste movement. The best time to search is right after clearing a room of enemies, when we have a safe turn to spare. Filch is the ideal searcher because his abilities make him better at finding useful items. Give found items to the mouse who benefits most: weapons to fighters, armor to the frontline, and utility items to whoever needs a boost.

walkthrough.title: Managing the Page Track
walkthrough.text: The page track is our real opponent. Everything we do should be measured against how much time it costs. Before each turn, glance at the cheese wheel and the page track. If the wheel is almost full, a surge is coming whether we like it or not. In that case, position the party near the next mouse hole so we can advance immediately after the surge. If the page track is getting low, it is time to abandon all nonessential fights and sprint for the objective. A lost chapter can be retried, but wasted time cannot be recovered.

walkthrough.title: Communication is Key
walkthrough.text: Mice and Mystics is a cooperative game, and the best teams communicate constantly. Before each turn, briefly discuss the plan as a group. Who should attack, who should heal, who should move toward the next room. Call out the cheese wheel status and page track position regularly. If someone spots a problem or an opportunity, speak up. The worst thing in a cooperative game is a player making a solo decision that hurts the whole team. Plan together, act together, and celebrate together.

summary.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
bullets: Don't split the party — isolated mice are vulnerable and can't share resources
Don't fight every enemy — unnecessary combat advances the cheese wheel
Don't waste cheese on minor abilities — save for bosses and emergencies
Don't skip search tokens — equipment makes later fights much easier

summary.title: Party Composition
bullets: Need at least 1 melee fighter (Collin) and 1 healer (Tilda)
Ranged support (Nez/Lily) adds safe damage from behind
Filch adds utility with stealth and searching bonuses
Balance frontline, healing, and ranged damage

summary.title: Cheese Management
bullets: Save cheese for critical moments — bosses, emergency heals, big spells
Keep 2+ cheese in reserve on strongest ability user
Tilda should always have healing cheese, Maginos should have spell cheese
Spend freely only when victory is within reach

summary.title: When to Fight and When to Run
bullets: Fight: enemies blocking path, clearing room to search, quick easy kills
Run: page track low, enemies in rooms you don't need, can scurry past
Every fight risks cheese → wheel → surge → page advance
Learning to disengage is a critical skill

summary.title: Movement and Positioning
bullets: Tank (Collin) leads, ranged 1-2 spaces behind, healer in the middle
Move as a formation — never let anyone wander off alone
Position to protect squishier mice from being targeted
Regroup near exits before exploring new rooms

summary.title: Search Strategy
bullets: Search when tokens are along the path — don't backtrack
Best time to search: after clearing a room of enemies
Filch is the ideal searcher — his abilities improve search results
Distribute items to the mouse who benefits most

summary.title: Managing the Page Track
bullets: Measure every decision against time cost on the page track
Cheese wheel nearly full → position near next mouse hole
Page track low → abandon nonessential fights, sprint for objective
Wasted time can't be recovered — lost chapters can be retried

summary.title: Communication is Key
bullets: Discuss the plan briefly before each turn
Call out cheese wheel and page track status regularly
Speak up about problems or opportunities
Plan together, act together — no solo hero decisions

walkthrough.title: Optimal Party Builds
walkthrough.text: The strongest party compositions exploit role synergies. Collin and Tilda form the core: Collin absorbs damage while Tilda keeps him healed, creating a nearly unkillable frontline. Add Nez for ranged support and item crafting, or Lily for pure ranged damage. Filch is a specialist: weaker in direct combat but excellent at finding and using search items. For harder chapters, consider doubling up on melee with Collin and Filch while keeping Tilda as a dedicated healer.

walkthrough.title: Cheese Economy Optimization
walkthrough.text: Advanced cheese management means thinking two rooms ahead. The ideal pattern is to build up cheese during easy fights in early rooms, then spend it during the climactic encounters. Collin should aim to enter boss rooms with at least three cheese for his strongest ability. Tilda needs two cheese at all times as a heal reserve. When the cheese wheel is about to surge anyway, deliberately trigger abilities before the reset so you get value from the surge you cannot prevent.

walkthrough.title: Speed Running Chapters
walkthrough.text: Some chapters can be completed much faster by minimizing combat. The key technique is the scurry chain: instead of attacking, each mouse scurries through rooms at double speed. Send Collin through the mouse hole first to absorb any entrance ambush, then scurry the rest of the party through on their turns. Skip search tokens in rooms that are not on the critical path. This strategy works best when the page track is running low and the objective is close.

walkthrough.title: Controlling the Cheese Wheel
walkthrough.text: Experienced players learn to predict and manipulate surges. Count the cheese on the wheel and estimate how many minion rolls it will take to trigger a surge. If a surge is inevitable this round, use the time to position for the aftermath rather than trying to prevent it. Conversely, if we can eliminate minions before they attack, we reduce the cheese flowing to the wheel. Quick kills are better than prolonged fights because each combat round is another chance for cheese to accumulate.

walkthrough.title: Advanced Combat Tactics
walkthrough.text: Coordinate attacks to focus fire on one enemy at a time rather than spreading damage across multiple targets. A dead enemy deals zero damage and generates zero cheese, while a wounded enemy at one health is just as dangerous as a full-health one. Use Collin to engage first and draw attacks, then have Nez or Lily finish targets from range. Tilda should only attack if there are no healing needs and the extra damage would kill an enemy. Every minion eliminated early is one less source of cheese wheel pressure.

walkthrough.title: Item Distribution Strategy
walkthrough.text: Equipment found through searching should go to the mouse who maximizes its value. Weapons always go to your primary attacker, usually Collin. Armor and shields go to the mouse who takes the most hits, also usually Collin. Support items like healing trinkets go to Tilda to amplify her healing ability. If Filch finds a stealth or thievery item, he should keep it. Never let a great item sit on a mouse who will not use it just because they found it. Pass items freely and think about team optimization.

walkthrough.title: Campaign Progression Tips
walkthrough.text: Items carry over between chapters, so think about the long game. An item that seems mediocre now might be critical in a later chapter. Never discard carried items between chapters unless you are at your carry limit. When choosing level-up abilities, pick ones that complement your team's weaknesses rather than doubling down on strengths. If Collin already hits hard, give him a defensive ability instead of more attack power. Build a well-rounded team across the campaign.

walkthrough.title: Boss Fight Preparation
walkthrough.text: Boss encounters are the climax of most chapters, and preparation is everything. Before entering a boss room, make sure every mouse has at least two cheese tokens. Ensure Tilda has a heal ready. Clear any remaining minions in the previous room so the boss fight is isolated. Position the party near the entrance so everyone can engage on the same turn. The worst thing in a boss fight is arriving one mouse at a time. Enter as a team, focus fire on the boss, and use abilities decisively rather than holding them back.

summary.title: Optimal Party Builds
bullets: Core: Collin (tank) + Tilda (healer) = nearly unkillable frontline
Add Nez for ranged + crafting, or Lily for pure ranged damage
Filch: weaker combat but excels at searching and utility
Hard chapters: consider double melee (Collin + Filch) with Tilda

summary.title: Cheese Economy Optimization
bullets: Build cheese in easy rooms, spend in boss rooms
Collin: enter boss fights with 3+ cheese; Tilda: keep 2 cheese reserve
If surge is inevitable, use abilities before the reset
Think two rooms ahead when spending cheese

summary.title: Speed Running Chapters
bullets: Scurry chain: skip actions, move at double speed through rooms
Collin enters first to absorb ambush, party follows
Skip non-critical search tokens
Best when page track is low and objective is close

summary.title: Controlling the Cheese Wheel
bullets: Count cheese on wheel → estimate turns until surge
Surge inevitable → position for aftermath instead of prevention
Eliminate minions quickly to reduce cheese flow
Quick kills > prolonged fights (fewer cheese-generating rolls)

summary.title: Advanced Combat Tactics
bullets: Focus fire: kill one enemy at a time, never spread damage
Dead enemy = 0 damage + 0 cheese; wounded enemy = full threat
Collin engages first → ranged finishers (Nez/Lily)
Tilda attacks only if no heals needed AND would finish a kill

summary.title: Item Distribution Strategy
bullets: Weapons → primary attacker (Collin), armor → frontline tank
Healing items → Tilda, stealth items → Filch
Never hoard items on the finder — pass to whoever maximizes value
Team optimization > individual loadouts

summary.title: Campaign Progression Tips
bullets: Items carry over — don't discard unless at carry limit
Level-up abilities: shore up weaknesses, don't double down on strengths
Mediocre items now may be critical in later chapters
Build a well-rounded team across the full 11-chapter campaign

summary.title: Boss Fight Preparation
bullets: Enter boss room with all mice having 2+ cheese
Clear minions in previous room before boss encounter
Position party at entrance so everyone engages on same turn
Focus fire on boss, use abilities decisively — don't hold back

entries.definition: The number of dice a hero rolls when making a melee attack. Shown on the hero card. Swords rolled count as hits against the target.
The number of dice rolled when defending against an attack. Each shield symbol rolled blocks one incoming hit. Both heroes and minions have defense values.
The number of spaces a mouse can move on its turn. Can also be used for the Scurry action, which grants a second movement equal to speed instead of taking an action.
The amount of damage a mouse can take before being captured. Tracked on the hero card. When health reaches zero, the mouse is captured and placed on the page track.
Prince turned mouse. The warrior of the group with the highest battle value and strong melee abilities. Best suited as the party's frontline tank and primary damage dealer.
Court wizard turned mouse. Casts powerful spells fueled by cheese tokens. Strong offensive abilities but lower defense. Keep him behind the frontline and supply him with cheese.
Healer mouse who can restore health to allies by spending cheese. Nearly essential for party survival. Keep her close to wounded allies and always maintain a cheese reserve for emergency heals.
Tinker mouse with ranged attack capabilities. Can craft equipment and support the party from range using bow attacks. Versatile and safe to play from behind the frontline.
Scamp and thief mouse. Stealthy with surprise attack abilities. Weaker in direct combat but excellent at searching for items and using trickery. Good utility pick for the party.
Archer mouse with strong ranged attacks using bow symbols. Deals consistent damage from a safe distance. Pairs well with a melee frontliner like Collin.
A die face representing a melee hit. Each sword rolled during an attack deals one point of damage to the target, reduced by shields rolled in defense.
A die face representing a defensive block. Each shield rolled when defending cancels one incoming hit. Both heroes and minions roll shields.
A die face representing a ranged hit. Used by mice with ranged weapons like Lily and Nez. Each bow rolled counts as one hit against the target at range.

A die face that generates cheese tokens. When heroes roll cheese, they collect it personally. When minions roll cheese, it goes to the shared cheese wheel. Cheese appears on dice alongside combat symbols.

The shared tracker that fills with cheese from minion dice rolls. When it reaches its limit, it resets to zero and triggers a minion surge plus a page track advance. This is the primary timer mechanic threatening the party.
The chapter's countdown timer. Advances when the cheese wheel fills and triggers a surge. If the page marker reaches the final position before the objective is complete, the chapter is lost.
When the cheese wheel fills, new minions appear on the board as specified by the chapter. The type and number of minions depends on the chapter and current room. Surges also advance the page track.
The turn order for heroes and minion groups within a round. Determined by initiative cards at the start of the chapter. Heroes and minion groups alternate turns based on this order.
Instead of taking a normal action on your turn, you may scurry: move a second time equal to your speed value. Useful for covering large distances or running past enemies without fighting.
An action taken on a space with a search token. Draw one card from the search deck to find items, equipment, or story events. The search token is then removed from the board.
Moving through a mouse hole or doorway to reveal the next room tile. The chapter instructions specify which tile to place and what minions and tokens appear in the new room.
Mice on the same space can freely exchange cheese tokens and trade equipped items during either mouse's turn. This enables team resource management and optimal item distribution.

When a mouse's health drops to zero, it is captured rather than eliminated. The captured mouse is placed on the page track and misses turns until rescued by teammates. If all mice are captured simultaneously, the chapter is lost.
Teammates can free a captured mouse by reaching specific locations or completing tasks described in the chapter rules. The rescued mouse returns with reduced health and rejoins the party.
Double-sided modular tiles that form the game board. Each chapter specifies which tiles to use and in what order. Tiles connect through mouse holes and doorways at their edges.
An exit point on a room tile that connects to another room. Moving through a mouse hole triggers exploration of the next tile. Some mouse holes are one-way or have special conditions.
A token placed on specific spaces in a room tile. When a mouse uses the search action on this space, they draw a search card. Tokens are removed after being searched.
Cards drawn when searching. Contain weapons, armor, trinkets, or story events. Found items can be equipped immediately or traded to another mouse on the same space.

Unique power cards specific to each mouse hero. Most require spending cheese tokens to activate. Abilities range from powerful attacks to heals to defensive maneuvers. New abilities can be gained between chapters.
Cards drawn when the page marker lands on certain positions on the page track. Encounters bring surprise enemies, story twists, or special events that add unpredictability to the chapter.
Cards used to determine turn order within each round. Each hero and each minion group has an initiative card. These are arranged to set the sequence of play.
Narrative cards for each chapter that describe the plot, objectives, and special rules. Read aloud during setup and at key story moments throughout the chapter.
The most common minion type. Rats are basic melee enemies with moderate attack and low defense. They appear frequently during surges and in newly explored rooms.
Fast-moving minion with low attack but hard to hit. Roaches are annoying because they generate cheese on the wheel while being difficult to pin down in combat.
A tougher minion with more health than rats. Centipedes deal significant damage and require focused attacks to bring down. Prioritize eliminating them quickly.
A dangerous minion that can trap mice with web abilities. Spiders restrict movement and can isolate party members. Free trapped allies quickly to maintain group cohesion.
A massive cat that serves as a recurring boss-level threat. Brodie has high health, powerful attacks, and can devastate the party. Requires coordinated team effort and ability usage to defeat.
The specific goal that must be completed to win each chapter. Objectives vary: rescue allies, reach locations, defeat bosses, find items. Always keep the objective in mind when making decisions.
The full story mode spanning eleven chapters. Items and some abilities carry over between chapters. The campaign tells the story of mice heroes trying to save their kingdom from an evil sorcerer.
